title: Finish the router to service-layer extraction (notes, auth)
summary: settings, health, and note-events already have service modules, but notes CRUD and auth logic still live in their routers. Extract them into notes_service.py and auth_service.py so routers stay thin and the business logic is directly testable.
motivation: Consistency with the established service-layer pattern (settings_service mirrors this) and thin, declarative routers. Salvaged from closed PR #8 (feature/service-layer-refactor).

included: Create app/services/notes_service.py holding notes create/update/delete/list orchestration, note-id generation, and ORM to schema conversion; routers/notes.py becomes dependency wiring plus service calls
Create app/services/auth_service.py holding login and token issuance plus get_user_by_username; routers/auth.py becomes dependency wiring plus service calls
Preserve every endpoint path, request/response schema, status code, transaction error handling, and the SSE stream wiring
excluded: Changing any API contract, response shape, or status code
Moving or changing the note-events SSE stream, broker, or LISTEN wiring (stays in the router/services already present)
Changing auth semantics or token behavior

design_facts.stack: FastAPI/Python 3.13 backend, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async. Existing service layer: services/settings_service.py, health_service.py, note_events.py, note_event_streams.py. notes and auth routers still carry business logic.

work_items.title: Extract notes business logic into notes_service
work_items.summary: Move notes create/update/delete/list orchestration, note-id generation, ORM to schema conversion, and transaction error handling out of routers/notes.py into a new services/notes_service.py; the router delegates. The SSE stream wiring stays in the router.

exposes.note: New services/notes_service.py functions for notes CRUD orchestration; routers/notes.py delegates, mirroring settings_service.

behavior: Notes CRUD endpoints keep identical paths, request/response schemas, and status codes
Ownership checks (404 for another user's note) and SQLAlchemyError rollback-to-500 handling are preserved in the service
The GET /notes/events SSE stream and its broker wiring are unchanged

signals: destructive-op:notes deletion logic relocated to the service

oracle.command: make test-backend-lint && make test-backend-format && make test-backend-types && cd backend && uv run pytest tests/integration/notes/test_notes_router.py tests/integration/notes/test_note_tags_api.py tests/unit/routers/test_notes_transaction_errors.py tests/unit/notes/test_notes_utils.py -v
assertions: Notes create/update/delete/list keep the same status codes, response shapes, and ownership 404s.
Transaction errors still roll back and return HTTP 500 with the existing message.
routers/notes.py delegates the CRUD business logic to notes_service.

work_items.title: Extract auth business logic into auth_service
work_items.summary: Move login and token issuance plus get_user_by_username out of routers/auth.py into a new services/auth_service.py; the router delegates.

exposes.note: New services/auth_service.py functions for login/token issuance and get_user_by_username; routers/auth.py delegates.

behavior: Login, refresh, /auth/me, and logout endpoints keep identical paths, schemas, and status codes
Credential verification and token issuance behavior are unchanged
get_user_by_username is a reusable service function

signals: sensitive-zone:auth login and token issuance path

oracle.command: make test-backend-lint && make test-backend-format && make test-backend-types && cd backend && uv run pytest tests/integration/auth/test_auth_router.py tests/unit/auth/test_auth.py tests/unit/auth/test_dependencies.py -v
assertions: Login with valid and invalid credentials returns the existing tokens and status codes.
/auth/me, refresh, and logout endpoints behave identically after the extraction.
routers/auth.py delegates login/token issuance and user lookup to auth_service.
